exercises.title: Base64 Round Trip
exercises.description: Encode a short string to Base64 and decode it back.
exercises.command:
python3 - <<'PY'
import base64
s = b'hello world'
print(base64.b64encode(s).decode())
print(base64.b64decode(base64.b64encode(s)).decode())
PY
exercises.expected_outcome: You see the encoded text and the original string restored.

exercises.title: Inspect JSON Size
exercises.description: Compare the byte size of a small JSON payload with its pretty-printed form.
exercises.command:
python3 - <<'PY'
import json
obj = {'name':'learn','count':3,'tags':['a','b']}
print(len(json.dumps(obj).encode()))
print(len(json.dumps(obj, indent=2).encode()))
PY
exercises.expected_outcome: The pretty-printed form should be larger than the compact form.

exercises.title: Gzip a Text File
exercises.description: Compress a text file and compare original and compressed sizes.
exercises.expected_outcome: The gzipped file should usually be smaller for text-heavy input.

exercises.title: Serialize and Parse Records
exercises.description: Write a small serializer for a struct or dict and parse it back.
exercises.expected_outcome: A record should round-trip without losing fields.

exercises.title: Versioned Schema
exercises.description: Build a versioned JSON or binary schema that can read both old and new records.
exercises.expected_outcome: Old records remain readable after adding a new optional field.

exercises.title: Compression Tradeoff Test
exercises.description: Benchmark raw transfer versus compressed transfer for a sample payload.
exercises.expected_outcome: You can compare time saved on bandwidth with CPU spent compressing.
